meta.label: Lamb Shawarma (Oven Roast + Broil or Skillet)
meta.description: Levantine-style lamb shawarma with a bold warm-spice marinade, lemon, and garlic. Designed for home kitchens: roast on a sheet pan for browned edges, then broil or skillet-sear for shawarma-style char.

summary: Home-style lamb shawarma using a classic Levantine shawarma spice blend (cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, allspice/cinnamon) plus garlic and lemon. Works with lamb shoulder/leg cut into strips or cubes; roast hot for browning and finish with a quick broil or skillet sear for char. Serve in pita with tahini or garlic sauce, pickles, and salad.

yields.makes: About 1.0–1.2 kg cooked lamb (enough for 6 wraps or 6 bowls)

diet.notes: Use gluten-free pita or serve as a bowl for gluten-free. Omit yogurt (not used here) to keep dairy-free.

compat.notes: Primary method is oven roasting; broiler/skillet finish adds char.

foodSafetyNotes: Handle raw meat safely; avoid cross-contamination.
Cook lamb to your preferred doneness, but ensure surface browning for best texture and flavor.
If using leftover marinade as sauce, boil it first; do not use raw marinade that touched raw meat.
Cool leftovers quickly and refrigerate within 2 hours; reheat until steaming hot.

ingredients.label: Lamb shoulder or leg, cut into thin strips or 1-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: Shoulder gives best flavor. Trim excess surface fat if desired.

ingredients.label: Garlic cloves, finely grated or minced
Yellow onion, thinly sliced (optional)
ingredients.notes: Optional roasting bed; turns sweet and jammy.

steps.title: Make the shawarma marinade
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together lemon juice, olive oil, grated garlic, cumin, coriander, paprika, turmeric, allspice, cinnamon (optional), black pepper, salt, and red pepper flakes (optional).
Taste and adjust salt/lemon/spice; it should be boldly seasoned.

steps.title: Marinate the lamb
instructions: Add lamb and toss until fully coated.
Cover and refrigerate at least 4 hours (best 12–24 hours).
If short on time, marinate at room temperature for 45 minutes and cook promptly (flavor will be lighter).

steps.title: Preheat and set up the pan
instructions: Line a sheet pan with foil for easier cleanup.
Optional: spread sliced onion on the pan as a bed; add lemon slices if using.

steps.title: Roast until browned
instructions: Spread lamb in a single layer on the sheet pan (include onions if using). Leave space so it roasts rather than steams.
Roast 18–28 minutes, stirring once halfway, until browned in spots and cooked to your preference.
For thin strips, check early (around 15–18 minutes). For 1-inch cubes, expect closer to 22–28 minutes.

steps.title: Finish with char (recommended)
instructions: For shawarma-style edges, broil 2–4 minutes until browned/charred in spots (watch closely).
Alternatively, sear the roasted lamb quickly in a very hot skillet with a drizzle of oil for 1–3 minutes.

steps.title: Rest and toss with juices
instructions: Toss with pan juices and roasted onions for maximum flavor.

steps.title: Serve
instructions: Serve in warm pita or bowls with tahini sauce or garlic sauce, sliced tomatoes and cucumber, red onion (optional), pickles/pickled turnips, parsley, and hot sauce (optional).
Leftovers keep 3–4 days refrigerated. Reheat in a hot skillet for best texture.

notes: Cut choice: Shoulder/leg works great. If using lamb loin chops or tenderloin, reduce cook time to avoid overcooking.
Char matters: That last broil/skillet sear gives the signature shawarma edge and aroma.
Make-ahead: Cooked lamb reheats best in a hot skillet with a tiny splash of water to loosen spices, then sear dry.

variations.label: Skewer-style lamb shawarma
changes: Thread marinated lamb onto skewers and grill or broil, turning, until browned and cooked through.

variations.label: Extra aromatic
changes: Add 1 tsp ground cardamom to the spice blend.
Add 1–2 tsp sumac to the marinade for extra tang.

variations.label: Spicier shawarma
changes: Increase red pepper flakes to 2 g (about 1 tsp) or add 1 tsp cayenne.
Serve with a harissa-style hot sauce.
